creator: genie@geniechka.ru
text:
Hi!

Originally there was no user/group but they were created in the previous attempts and now all is ok:

[root@s1 /usr/ports/security/logcheck]# grep logcheck /etc/master.passwd
logcheck:*:915:915::0:0:Logcheck system account:/var/db/logcheck:/usr/local/bin/bash
[root@s1 /usr/ports/security/logcheck]# grep logcheck /etc/group
logcheck:*:915:

I tried installing it also on another system (virtual machine) â almost clean FreeBSD 8.2 upgraded to 8.4 â and the problem occurs there as well, but not on another server with FreeBSD 9.1.

I added your patch but it did not produce any additional output. Actually the error occurs just after PREINSTALL phase. I added âd switch to make and got this:

pw: user 'logcheck' already exists
---> Created user "logcheck".
install -d -o logcheck -g logcheck  /var/db/logcheck /var/run/logcheck
install: logcheck: Invalid argument
*** Error code 67

Donât know how that command is called =)

Thank you for your help
Eugene
